commands.review_summary:
The PR routes Codex-native command/file/MCP transcript results through codex tool-result middleware and adds a Codex event-projector regression test.

PR surface: Source +37, Tests +73. Total +110 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: the PR sends `details: {}` into middleware while Tokenjuice 0.8.0 requires completed/failed exec-style details before compaction. The linked issue also contains a concrete live `openclaw agent` reproduction and observed raw stored result shape.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The contributor needs to repair the middleware result contract and add real behavior proof; maintainers may also choose the open sibling PR if it becomes the canonical landing path.

target.title: fix(codex): pipe native bash results through tool-result middleware (#88537)
target.body:
## Summary

- Routes Codex-native bash/commandExecution, apply_patch/fileChange, and mcpToolCall results through the tool-result middleware pipeline before writing to the mirrored transcript
- Fixes TokenJuice compaction being silently skipped for all native Codex tool calls

## Motivation

Closes #88537.

`commandExecution` (bash) items are handled in `event-projector.ts` via `recordNativeToolTranscriptResult`, which wrote the raw output directly into the transcript without invoking the `AgentToolResultMiddlewareRunner`. OpenClaw dynamic tool calls (e.g. `browser`, `exec`) went through `CodexDynamicToolBridge.handleToolCall` in `dynamic-tools.ts`, which correctly calls `middlewareRunner.applyToolResultMiddleware` — so those were compacted. Native tools were entirely bypassed.

The fix constructs a `nativeToolMiddlewareRunner` (runtime: `'codex'`) in `CodexAppServerEventProjector` and awaits it inside the newly-async `recordNativeToolTranscriptResult` before building the transcript message. The runner is a zero-cost lazy no-op when no middleware is registered, so there is no performance impact for users without TokenJuice or other tool-result middleware plugins installed.

## Verification

- TypeScript: `npx tsc --noEmit --project extensions/codex/tsconfig.json` — **6 errors, 0 new** (all pre-existing unresolvable SDK module paths in this dev environment; identical error set before and after the patch)
- New Vitest unit test added: `event-projector.test.ts` — installs a mock middleware in the active plugin registry, fires a `commandExecution` `item/completed` notification, and asserts:
  1. the middleware was invoked with `toolName === 'bash'`
  2. the compacted text (not the raw `aggregatedOutput`) appears in the mirrored transcript `messagesSnapshot`
- Regression baseline: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex.config.ts` — **85 failed before and after** (pre-existing environment issue: `@openclaw/fs-safe/config` not resolvable without full monorepo install)
- Did NOT change: the `dynamic-tools.ts` middleware runner path for OpenClaw dynamic tools; `recordNativeToolTranscriptCall` (tool-call side); the `handleItemCompleted` dispatch logic
target.author: bartok9
